title: erodium cicutarium proles cariotii
intro: Erodium cicutarium proles cariotii, also known as the redstem filaree, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the geranium family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various gastrointestinal issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also known to have health benefits, including lowering blood pressure and reducing inflammation in the body. Erodium cicutarium proles cariotii can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds in a solvent, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
